serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one skewer containing approximately three fishballs, weighing about 45 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from 'Fishball, boiled' (ID: 01001001) from HPB Singapore, as it represents a common preparation method for fishballs on a skewer.

gi_source.title: Fish cake, steamed
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated from 'Fish cake, steamed' (GI 50) as fishballs are similar processed fish paste products with comparable starch content. No direct entry for fishball found in the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume fishball skewers in moderation due to their moderate-to-high sodium content (~400 mg per 100 g), which can contribute to elevated blood pressure with regular intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fishball skewers have a low estimated glycemic index (~50) and moderate carbohydrate content (~12 g per 100 g), making them a relatively suitable snack for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in appropriate portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate sodium and low saturated fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While low in saturated fat (~0.4 g per 100 g), the moderate-to-high sodium content of fishball skewers warrants mindful consumption for individuals with cardiovascular disease to help manage blood pressure and overall cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fishball skewers are a popular street food and snack in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. They consist of small, bouncy fishballs made from fish paste, starch, and seasonings, threaded onto a bamboo skewer. The fishballs are typically boiled or lightly fried, offering a mild, savory flavor and a springy, chewy texture. They are commonly served plain or with dipping sauces such as chili sauce or sweet sauce. Old Chang Kee, a well-known Singaporean snack chain, markets them as 'Fishball OnStik'. Nutritionally, fishball skewers provide a moderate source of protein (~10 g per 100 g) from the fish, with moderate carbohydrates (~12 g per 100 g) and low fat (~2 g per 100 g), especially when boiled. However, they can be relatively high in sodium (~400 mg per 100 g) due to processing and added seasonings. Their estimated low glycemic index (~50) makes them a reasonable snack choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes in moderation. Those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the sodium content.
